healthcare.notes: Healthcare shows higher ethics/output evaluation scores due to clinical AI governance experience, but lower technology ecosystem scores.

professional_services.notes: Professional services leads on culture and workforce capability. Consulting firms significantly above median.

technology.notes: Technology sector leads all sectors. Wide variance between AI-native companies and legacy tech.

manufacturing.notes: Manufacturing lags most sectors. Advanced manufacturers (automotive, aerospace) significantly above median.

public_sector.notes: Public sector shows higher ethics scores but lower technology and culture scores. Central government above local government.

education.notes: Education sector shows higher AI interaction scores due to academic familiarity with AI tools.

vision_statement.prompt_template:
You are an expert HR strategy consultant writing for a CHRO audience.

Context:
- Organisation: {{company_name}} ({{sector}}, {{org_size}} employees)
- Current HR AI maturity: {{current_maturity_score}}/10
- Target maturity: {{target_maturity_score}}/10
- Ambition tier: {{ambition_tier}}
- Primary business driver: {{primary_business_driver}}
- Strategy horizon: {{strategy_horizon_months}} months

Write a 2-3 sentence HR AI vision statement for this organisation.

Requirements:
- Name the sector and business context explicitly
- State what HR will be able to do differently as a result of this strategy
- Include one specific, falsifiable outcome (a number, a capability, a behaviour)
- Write in third person ("{{company_name}}'s HR function will...")
- Do NOT use generic phrases like "world-class", "best-in-class", "cutting-edge", "transformative journey", "unlock potential", "empower employees", or "leverage AI"
- Do NOT write a values statement — write a capability statement
- Do NOT mention "AI" in every sentence — focus on what the capability enables

Output: Vision statement only. No preamble, no bullet points.

context.primary_business_driver: Reduce time-to-hire and improve manager quality

golden_example.output: Meridian Retail Group's HR function will operate as a data-driven partner to the business — using AI to cut time-to-hire by 40%, give every store manager a weekly people insight they can act on, and free HR professionals from administrative work to focus on the decisions that require human judgement. By the end of 2027, no significant people decision at Meridian will be made without a data point behind it.

guiding_principles.prompt_template:
You are an expert HR strategy consultant writing for a CHRO audience.

Context:
- Organisation: {{company_name}} ({{sector}}, {{org_size}} employees)
- Ambition tier: {{ambition_tier}}
- Primary business driver: {{primary_business_driver}}
- Top 3 capability gaps: {{top_capability_gaps}}
- Top regulatory risk: {{top_regulatory_risk}}

Write exactly 5 guiding principles for this organisation's HR AI strategy.

Requirements:
- Each principle must be specific to this organisation's context — not generic
- Each principle must include an explicit trade-off or constraint (what this means we WON'T do)
- Each principle must be falsifiable — it should be possible to test whether the organisation is following it
- Write as short, declarative statements (max 25 words each)
- Do NOT use generic principles like "put people first", "be ethical", "embrace innovation"
- Do NOT repeat the vision statement
- Principles should address: governance, capability, data, change, and outcomes

Format: Return as a JSON array of objects with keys: title (max 6 words), statement (max 25 words), trade_off (max 20 words).

context.primary_business_driver: Reduce time-to-hire and improve manager quality
context.top_capability_gaps: AI Workflow Design, Workforce AI Readiness
context.top_regulatory_risk: AI-assisted CV screening without bias audit

output.title: Human sign-off on every hire
output.statement: No candidate will be rejected by AI alone — every shortlist requires a trained recruiter to review and approve.
output.trade_off: This means slower time-to-shortlist than fully automated competitors.

output.title: Data quality before analytics
output.statement: We will not build predictive models on data we cannot trust. Audit and remediation precede every analytics initiative.
output.trade_off: This means delaying attrition modelling until Q3 2026.

output.title: Managers own the insight
output.statement: People analytics outputs go to the manager accountable for the decision — not to HR as an intermediary.
output.trade_off: This means investing in manager AI literacy before dashboard rollout.

output.title: Transparency to employees first
output.statement: Before deploying any AI tool that affects employees, we will tell them what it does, why, and how to challenge it.
output.trade_off: This means some AI deployments will require consultation periods.

output.title: Measure what changes
output.statement: Every AI initiative has a defined business metric. If we cannot measure it, we will not fund it.
output.trade_off: This means some qualitative improvements will not be prioritised.

out_of_scope.prompt_template:
You are an expert HR strategy consultant writing for a CHRO audience.

Context:
- Organisation: {{company_name}} ({{sector}}, {{org_size}} employees)
- Ambition tier: {{ambition_tier}}
- Strategy horizon: {{strategy_horizon_months}} months
- In-scope initiatives: {{initiative_list}}
- Top regulatory risk: {{top_regulatory_risk}}
- Maturity gap: {{maturity_gap}} points

Write 4-5 explicit out-of-scope items for this HR AI strategy.

Requirements:
- Each item must be a specific AI use case or capability that is NOT in this strategy
- Each item must include a brief reason why it is out of scope (capacity, risk, sequencing, or strategic choice)
- Items should be genuinely tempting — things a CHRO might reasonably want to do but has chosen not to
- Do NOT include trivially out-of-scope items (e.g., "we won't build our own AI model")
- At least one item should reference a regulatory or risk reason
- At least one item should reference a sequencing reason (not yet, not now)
- Write in first person plural ("We will not...")

Format: Return as a JSON array of objects with keys: item (max 15 words), reason (max 25 words).

context.initiative_list: AI Literacy, AI Ethics Framework, HR Process Automation, People Analytics Dashboard, Predictive Attrition
context.top_regulatory_risk: AI-assisted CV screening without bias audit

output.item: We will not deploy AI in performance management decisions
output.reason: Insufficient governance maturity and legal risk. Revisit in 2027 after governance programme is operational.

output.item: We will not use AI to monitor employee productivity or wellbeing signals
output.reason: Employee trust and GDPR risk outweigh the benefit at current maturity. Requires consultation with employee representatives first.

output.item: We will not build a custom AI model for HR
output.reason: Commercial AI tools meet our needs at lower cost and risk. Custom model development is a Phase 3 consideration.

output.item: We will not roll out AI tools to the business before HR has adopted them
output.reason: HR must be credible practitioners before advising the business. Internal adoption precedes external advocacy.

output.item: We will not automate candidate rejection without human review
output.reason: EU AI Act high-risk classification and Equality Act 2010 exposure. Human review is a non-negotiable control.

risk_statement.prompt_template:
You are an expert HR strategy consultant writing for a CHRO audience.

Context:
- Organisation: {{company_name}} ({{sector}}, {{org_size}} employees)
- Initiative: {{initiative_name}}
- Risk trigger: {{risk_trigger}}
- Risk severity: {{risk_severity}}
- Recommended action: {{recommended_action}}

Write a concise risk statement for this delivery risk.

Requirements:
- State the risk in plain English (what could go wrong, not a generic category)
- Include the likely consequence if the risk materialises
- Write for a CHRO who will present this to a board — be direct, not hedged
- Max 3 sentences
- Do NOT use jargon like "risk appetite", "risk tolerance", "risk register"
- Do NOT use passive voice

Output: Risk statement only. No preamble.

golden_example.output: If Meridian deploys AI CV screening before the bias audit is complete, the tool may produce discriminatory shortlists — creating exposure under the Equality Act 2010 and the EU AI Act. An employment tribunal claim or ICO investigation at this stage would delay the entire talent acquisition programme by 12-18 months and damage Meridian's employer brand.

executive_narrative.prompt_template:
You are an expert HR strategy consultant writing for a CHRO audience.

Context:
- Organisation: {{company_name}} ({{sector}}, {{org_size}} employees)
- Current HR AI maturity: {{current_maturity_score}}/10
- Target maturity: {{target_maturity_score}}/10
- Gap: {{maturity_gap}} points
- Ambition tier: {{ambition_tier}}
- Number of initiatives: {{initiative_count}}
- Strategy horizon: {{strategy_horizon_months}} months
- Top 3 initiatives: {{top_initiatives}}
- Total investment envelope: {{investment_envelope}}

Write a 150-200 word executive narrative for this HR AI strategy.

Requirements:
- Open with the current situation (where HR is today)
- State the ambition (where HR needs to be)
- Name the 2-3 most important initiatives and why they matter
- Close with the investment ask and the primary business outcome
- Write in third person
- Be specific — use the numbers provided
- Do NOT use generic phrases (see forbidden list)
- Do NOT repeat the vision statement verbatim

Output: Narrative only. No preamble, no headers.

context.top_initiatives: AI Literacy Programme, People Analytics Dashboard, Predictive Attrition Modelling

golden_example.output: Meridian's HR function currently operates at 4.2/10 on the AiQ capability scale — below the retail sector median of 4.8 and significantly below the 6.8 required to deliver the Innovators ambition. The gap is concentrated in AI Workflow Design and Workforce AI Readiness, where HR professionals can use AI tools but cannot yet redesign processes around them or develop others. This 18-month strategy closes that gap through seven initiatives. The AI Literacy Programme builds the foundation; the People Analytics Dashboard gives managers the visibility they currently lack; and Predictive Attrition Modelling converts that data into decisions that reduce regrettable attrition. The total investment envelope is £280,000-£420,000 over 18 months. The primary return is a 40% reduction in time-to-hire and a 15-25% reduction in regrettable attrition — outcomes worth £1.2-2.1m annually at Meridian's current attrition cost.
